The user wants a PDDL plan for a specific problem instance.
The problem is about "folding" a string of nodes.
The goal is to move the nodes from their initial configuration to a target configuration.
The domain defines actions for rotating a string at a specific node.
The rotation process involves:
1. `rotate`: Changes the heading of the pivot node and starts the process.
2. `rotate-first-pass`: Updates the heading and frees the old position of the next node in the string, moving the cursor.
3. `rotate-first-pass-end`: Finishes the first pass when the end node is reached.
4. `rotate-second-pass`: Updates the position of the next node in the string based on the new heading.
5. `rotate-second-pass-end`: Finishes the second pass.

The goal requires changing the shape from a straight vertical line to a spiral.
The rotation action `rotate` takes a node `?n` and a rotation `?r`.
It changes the heading of `?n` from `?fromdir` to `?todir`.
It sets `node-first-pass-next` to `?n` (the pivot) and `?r`.
Then `rotate-first-pass` iterates through the connected nodes starting from the neighbor of `?n`.
It updates the heading of the neighbor to the new direction relative to the rotation.
It frees the old position of the neighbor.
It moves the cursor to the next node.
When the end node is reached, `rotate-first-pass-end` sets `node-second-pass-next` to `?n`.
Then `rotate-second-pass` iterates through the connected nodes starting from the neighbor of `?n`.
It sets the position of the neighbor based on the new heading of the previous node.
It marks the position as occupied.
It moves the cursor to the next node.
When the end node is reached, `rotate-second-pass-end` clears `rotating`.
